entry.title: Re: How to use joins in Webi Query Builder
entry.content:
You are correct, joins are not supported.  However, you might be able to use the "BI on BI" universe to get it.  If that doesn't work, you'll have to either look at a third-party tool - APOS and Wiisdom have good ones - or write code using either the Java or .NET SDK to get this info.

-Dell

entry.title: HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT
entry.content:
Hello everybody, my name is Agostina, I am from Italy.  

I have worked with SAP as a Personnel Administrator for 8 years but I am a little lost on how to certificate my knowledge and also to improve it.

I have complete the "Getting started with SAP HCM and HCM Payroll" course, but I am not sure about how to continue.

My specific interest is more in Personnel Administration, can you give me a clue?

Wich are the certifications most important? 

Thank you all in advance!

Agostina

entry.title: BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE not updating WBS element
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a development that requires me to create a new PR and update the WBS element based on user input. I am using the BAPI BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE to do the same.

The BAPI runs successfully and all fields are created/populated except for the WBS element.

Please find some of my code snippets below:

l_it_praccount TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiebkn.   "Table Declaration

APPEND VALUE #( "res_doc = abap_false   "Populating Data
                      g_l_acct = <fs_deep>-sakto
                      preq_item = <fs_deep>-bnfpo
                      cost_ctr = <fs_deep>-kostl
                      wbs_elem = <fs_deep>-ps_psp_pnr   "WBS Element
                      distr_perc = <fs_deep>-vproz
                       )  TO l_it_praccount.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE'   "Passing Table to BAPI
            EXPORTING
              skip_items_with_error          = abap_true
              automatic_source               = abap_true
            IMPORTING
              number                         = lv_number
            TABLES
              requisition_items              = l_it_pritem[]
              requisition_account_assignment = l_it_praccount[]   "Table Mapping
*             REQUISITION_ITEM_TEXT          =
*             REQUISITION_LIMITS             =
*             REQUISITION_CONTRACT_LIMITS    =
*             REQUISITION_SERVICES           =
*             REQUISITION_SRV_ACCASS_VALUES  =
              return                         = lt_return[]
*             REQUISITION_SERVICES_TEXT      =
              requisition_addrdelivery       = l_it_item[]
*             EXTENSIONIN                    =
            .

Any ideas on where I might be going wrong?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content:
@bill_froelich 

I’m speechless... incredible! Here's what I did to solve the issue:

I went to downloadOffline.action and InitializeOffline.action and added the expand I needed. After doing that, the previously blank fields were populated with the expected data.

Now I can see the data in the Object Header.

Thanks a lot! I'm learning a lot with you and all community's posts and members.

As of now i used 24 copy operators to bring all months to the current month then have another new key figure to total all the actuals. I want to use the threshold in the XYZ Segmentation but I am having challenges in creating or choosing the right attribute. If you have any tips regarding this, please advise. Thank you so much

Regards,

Rafaelle

entry.title: SAP Build Apps and SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi, I have completed the mission Create an Application with SAP Build Apps, but I was wondering how can I connect to a SAP HANA  Cloud Public system, whether is a trial account or my company's dev tenant. 

Similar to the exercise in the mission, I would like to create an OData integration and connect to Overview | Outbound Delivery (A2X) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub  

When I enter the SANDBOX URL

https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV;v=0002
and test the service, nothing happens...

Regards,

AxI

entry.title: SAP RAP: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I need your help with one thing. 

I am currently implementing a “managed” RAP Fiori application and have encountered the following error in the course of my implementation:

 

Runtime Errors: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT

Statement "CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK" is not allowed with this status.

The statements
- CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK
- CALL FUNCTION IN BACKGROUND TASK/UNIT
- PERFORM ON COMMIT/ROLLBACK
are not allowed outside of the "Save" method.


I don't have much experience in integrating existing SAP ABAP classes into my RAP application, as I have only been performing operations using EML. In this case and also in future transactions I will not always be able to avoid developing everything greenfield, so I have to use existing code.

Stupid question, but can someone explain the error to me exactly?
It seems that I can't write anything to the database outside of the “Save” method. Which can't always be avoided, especially if I use standard code.

How can this be prevented in the future? Do RAP applications then have to be implemented exclusively “unmanaged”? We haven't had much experience with this yet.

Many thanks for your help

regards,

Lars

entry.title: Re: EmployeeDataBundleMaintainRequest_sync - Flag MainBankIndicator is ignored
entry.content: Is there a solution for this?

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Data Exchange Launched as Generally Available!
entry.content:
Are you struggling to measure and assess your Scope 3 emissions to meet regulatory requirements and achieve your company's net-zero goal?
As a buyer, do you find it challenging to collect and manage product carbon footprint data from your suppliers in a simplified and standardized way?
Are you a supplier tired of filling out different questionnaires, excel sheets, and answering emails to deliver sustainability data to your customers? If so, SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is the solution tailored to meet your needs.

What is SAP Sustainability Data Exchange?

In short, SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is a Software-as-a-Service solution based on SAP BTP that facilitates standardized data requests and sharing of Scope 3 emissions between supply chain partners. It also assists organizations in managing their own product carbon footprint data as well as that of their business partners.

What makes it different?

What makes SAP Sustainability Data Exchange different? It is all about standardization and network collaboration.

The cloud solution is integrated into SAP Business Network to leverage Trading Partner Directory and search capability for common registration, profile, and invitation process. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is industry agnostic, supports global standards as well as industry framework such as WBCSD PACT and Catena-X. It is officially listed as certified app for product carbon footprint exchange for Catena-X data space.  

Additionally, through out-of-the-box integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, organizations can truly realize carbon accounting on a transactional level, moving from averages to actuals.

Curious and want to learn more?

Visit SAP.com/SDX  for more exciting product information. We are now supporting digital sales experience! You can also find out more detailed guides on Help Portal and the future capabilities on the Roadmap Explorer.

Partners can also access the latest assets on the Partner Portal.

See how our reference customer WITTE Automotive talk about their decarbonization journey with SAP Sustainability solutions. 

Thank you to our customers, partners, colleagues, and the core SAP Sustainability Data Exchange team that have been and continue to contribute to its success! 👏 💚

**************************

Hi, 👋 I'm Leyi Liu, have been working with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange since almost the very beginning, now since October 2024 officially as Product Marketing Lead. I'm looking forward to sharing more updates and news on the product with you in the future. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Odata - Lock table entries till DEQUEd explicitely
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am using OData protocol and my requirement is table entries are to be locked unless explicitly DEQUEd  by FM. Since OData is stateless protocol , lock is retained till control exists in backend. 

I guess in GUI we used achieve this by using SCOPE = 1 . Can anyone please confirm if we can still achieve using ENQUE FMs.

Below blog seems to be a solution but not sure if it can cause any performance issues or any other limitations

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-odata-soft-state-with-delayed-invocation-for-locking/ba-p/13467989

entry.title: Unable to Transport OIM Account
entry.content:
Hi Team

 

We are unable to transport the OIM changes for GL Account 

Account balance is equal to zero, but the account contains postings


Diagnosis
You have changed the Open Item Management option (SKB1-XOPVW). However, the general ledger account contains postings for the current fiscal year, the prior year, or the year preceding the prior year.
Procedure
Changing the setting for this option merely changes account control. Items posted up to this date are not automatically switched to the changed setting.
If this option is subsequently activated, the existing line items are not converted to 'open items'.
We therefore recommend that you proceed as follows:
Use the job template Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts in program Schedule Accounting Data Changes to retroactively change the indicator for open item management. For more information, 
Message no. FH190


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Transport OIM Account
entry.content:
In the long text of error  FH190

You are recommended 
"Use the job template Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts in program Schedule Accounting Data Changes to retroactively change the indicator for open item management"

This is the solution. 

See also kba 3023775 - Error FH190 occurs when trying to change the open item management indicator

See help.sap.com documentation 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/658b0f8061424109936672e336a1bb0f.html?locale=en-US

 

Why can't I change the open item management setting in development system. 

First, it depends which kind of open item management you use. Different methods are possible for open item management, open item management by ledger group, and extended open item management. For example, for open item management, when you try to save a change to the Open Item Management indicator in the Manage G/L Account Master Data app, the development system communicates with the production system to check if it’s possible to make such a change. The production system checks and makes sure that the affected account has a zero balance and no postings. If the systems determine that the change is allowed, it is included in the next transport from the development system to the production system. If the systems determine the change isn’t allowed, you must schedule a job in the Schedule Accounting Data Changes app using the Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts job template. For more information about all three kinds of open item management, see Open Item Management for G/L Accounts.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Transforming legacy code to CAP with the help of GenAI

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content: Depending on the use case and data you can also included that entity set referred to by the navigation property rather than including it via the $expand. This will make all the data in that entity set available not just the expanded entries from the nav link.

entry.title: Re: TRM: Joining several identical flows within a period into one flow
entry.content:
Is this possible for a Interest Rate Swap? the thing there is that are two 1200 flows but in different direcctions (+ , -), i have done what you explained here but SAP is doing the sum of both flow without having the direcction into consideration. 

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Sales Contract to be consider in MRP in Public Cloud
entry.content:
A sales contract is a long-term agreement that outlines the special conditions negotiated with the customer, including the expected quantity or value of products to be sold to the customer over a specific period of time. The customer submits sales orders to release the product from the amount or value agreed in the sales contract.

The sales orders are customer requirements that system may take into account as a kind of demand. Depending on Material Requirements Planning (MRP) related parameters and configuration, this demand may be considered by the system for Material Requirements Planning (MRP).

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 


Good day to you!🤗

My name is Lily, and I’m currently working as an SAP consultant for a consulting firm in Vietnam. I have a background in Sales & Distribution, with some hands-on experience in SAP modules, particularly MM and SD. My passion for digital transformation and continuous learning motivates me to stay updated with the latest tools and techniques in the SAP ecosystem.

I’m excited about the opportunity to participate in the digital skills initiative, as I believe it aligns perfectly with my career goals. This program will not only enhance my current skill set but also empower me to contribute more effectively to my organization and clients.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to the opportunity to grow through this experience!

Warm regards,
Lily🤗

entry.title: when should an income statement GL account NOT have a matching cost element?
entry.content:
My company is using ERP 6.0.  Related to GL accounts for the income statement (aka profit and loss statement), almost all GL accounts have a matching cost element.

My question is what is the normal and expected use case for a P&L GL account to not have a cost element? 

adding more context, I am referring to primary cost elements, with one of 3 cost element categories
1 = primary costs/cost-reducing revenues

11 = Revenues

12 = Sales deduction

entry.title: SAP EWM OCA-UA interface for packaging
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have acquired a Packaging Machine for our warehouse to automate the packing process, and we require a OCA-UA interface for it. We need to establish a bi-directional interface for a packaging machine used for pallet wrapping. We will be sending handling unit (HU) information to the machine, and the machine will be sending HU dimensions back to SAP.

The manufacturer has recommended using the OCA-UA interface, and the license for the OCA UA server is included with the machine. Does anyone have experience with this type of interfacing technology for a packaging machine?

The SAP standard help directs us to SAP DMC, but obtaining a separate license for SAP DMC for just one machine in the warehouse seems impractical. Additionally, we would like to utilize the current available middleware SAP BTP Integration Suite with us.

Any insights on this topic would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks & Regards,

Sagar.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content: Add another Defining Request to the list that is the EntitySet that the cust_Inst1Nav points to and remove the $expand from the cust_Turmas query

Bruno

entry.title: Search Functionality Not Working in SuccessFactors Extension Application
entry.content:
We are building an application in CAP that extends SuccessFactors. No HANA or local database is used. We are using picklist API to get values for the filters in the application but found that the help values cannot be searched.

When applying search in the URL, the same thing happens. The search is not applied. No error is returned. We just get results as if no "search" term was used.

- http://localhost:5000/.../PL_ValueV2?$search=%22Street%20Address%22

OData Audit Log shows the request coming in and it includes the "search" parameter. Note that the dollar sign is missing. Is that the root cause? The CAP framework is leaving out the $?

I also tried to use the search in the API business hub. But again, the "search" is not applied and I get all results as if I didn't have a search added.

entry.title: Re: Sales Contract to be consider in MRP in Public Cloud
entry.content:
A sales contract is a long-term agreement that outlines the special conditions negotiated with the customer, including the expected quantity or value of products to be sold to the customer over a specific period of time. The customer submits sales orders to release the product from the amount or value agreed in the sales contract.

The sales orders are customer requirements that system may take into account as a kind of demand. Depending on Material Requirements Planning (MRP) related parameters and configuration, this demand may be considered by the system for Material Requirements Planning (MRP).

entry.title: Warehouse Transfer - issues to involve CO09 and BOP
entry.content:
We need to automate our warehouse sourcing process through the inter-warehouse transfer process. Today we have many problems that I can list below: -> Need for the supply team to supply the products that need to be transferred through a spreadsheet with item, origin deposit, destination deposit and quantity;-> Need for the storage team to make the transfer before the BOP reservation process;-> Origin deposit has a negative balance in the need because it ends up transferring stock that is reserved in Delivery;-> The whole process is very manual and requires a certain amount of headcounts to do; In order to implement a more agile process and without the human need to carry out such activities, I would like to know if SAP provides any transaction that allows us to execute a transfer between warehouses in a massive and scheduled way to execute daily supplies.

 

The idea is that I say to a certain warehouse example LV01 that the one in the XXX Center, Material: ABCD needs to have at least 3000 CXs and at most 5000 CXs and that it will be supplied by the deposit "ZZ01", that is, when the stock drops from 3000 CXs it will automatically transfer 2000 CXs withdrawing from LA01, however, he can only transfer the available quantity, that is, the quantity that is left over in ZZ01 where (Stock - Reserve = Available). Is there this standard process or just a customized one? Or something similar?

entry.content:
adding more context, I am referring to primary cost elements, with one of 3 cost element categories
1 = primary costs/cost-reducing revenues

11 = Revenues

12 = Sales deduction

entry.title: Re: FM ATP_BOP_API_REQUIREMENTS_RUN

entry.title: Re: SAP Dynamic Forms 1.0 Released Features
entry.content:
Updates: 

updated the product roadmap link.

Reply:

Yes dynamic forms can be used without using SSAM, it is a BTP service, the forms designed and created are links and is stored within BTP, they can be accessed online on the desktop.  linking it to a custom solution via custom development is possible.   

This is not a successor to SAP Interactive Forms by Adobe, no migration utilities are planned.  However, from a form design perspective, in 3.0 we are planning AI assisted form design capabilities that should help with the migration of legacy forms in pdf and paper format.

Thanks & Regards 

Mohana

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Schedule Publication API's: Getting Started Guide
entry.content:
Introduction to the Schedule Publication API

The Schedule Publication API is designed to facilitate the seamless integration of scheduling functionalities for selected reports within the SAP Analytics Cloud. This API enables partners and customers to programmatically manage the publication of reports, ensuring that all actions typically performed through the user interface can also be executed via API calls.

This API is particularly beneficial for customers seeking event-based scheduling, as it allows for the creation and triggering of publication schedules in response to specific events. By leveraging the capabilities of the Schedule Publication API, users can automate workflows, enhance operational efficiency, and ensure the timely delivery of critical reports. Whether it's initiating a schedule based on an external trigger or managing report publications on a recurring basis, this API provides the flexibility and control needed to streamline the reporting process.

API Guide

API Description Method Endpoint Usage

 

APIDescriptionMethodEndPointUsage
Template APIRetrieves a prefilled JSON payload for creating, updating, or deleting schedules.GET/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/templateGet this template, then modify the placeholder values based on the requirement and use it for Creating or updating the Schedule.
Create APIEstablish a new single or recurring Schedule Publication.POST/api/v1/storyPublication/schedulesSend your modified JSON payload from the template to create a schedule.
Update APIModify specific properties of an existing Schedule Publication.PATCH/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/scheduleIDMake modifications without needing to recreate the entire schedule.
Delete APIRemove a single schedule or a series of schedules.DELETE/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/scheduleIDClean up outdated or unnecessary schedules easily.
Get Schedule APIRetrieve details of a specific Schedule Publication by its ID.GET/api/v1/storyPublication/schedules/scheduleIDFetch the details of the specified schedule.

Note: Replace scheduleID with the ID of the schedule you want to update, delete, or retrieve.

API Body Payload Definition

The following payload will be returned by the template API:

 

{
    "storyId": "",
    "scheduleName": "",
    "recurrenceType": 1,
    "runNow": true,
    "scheduleMode": 0,
    "runOnceStartDate": "2024-10-14T15:02:56.111Z",
    "recurrence": [
        {
            "recurrenceMode": 3,
            "endMode": {
                "endModeRepetitions": 2,
                "endModeDate": null
            },
            "onMonday": false,
            "onTuesday": false,
            "onWednesday": false,
            "onThursday": false,
            "onFriday": false,
            "onSaturday": false,
            "onSunday": false,
            "firstRun": "2024-10-14T15:02:56.111Z",
            "dayCount": 30
        }
    ],
    "addStoryLink": true,
    "fileFormat": "PDF",
    "emailSubject": "",
    "emailMessage": "",
    "deliveryOption": 0,
    "timezoneFullName": "",
    "views": [
        {
            "bookmarkId": "STORY",
            "viewName": "view 1",
            "filename": "Sample",
            "fileSettings": {
                "selectedStoryPages": [],
                "includeAppendix": true,
                "appendixIncludeDate": true,
                "resolution": "1920,1080",
                "includeComments": false,
                "pageSize": "Auto"
            },
            "destinations": {
                "email": {
                    "extEmailUsr": [],
                    "internalEmailUsr": []
                }
            }
        }
    ],
    "xsTimeTaken": 53
}

 

Field Definitions

  • storyId: (string) Unique identifier for the story.
  • scheduleName: (string) Name of the schedule.
  • recurrenceType: (integer) Type of recurrence; defines how often the schedule runs. For a single schedule enter 1;  for a recurring schedule enter 2.
  • runNow: (boolean) Indicates whether the schedule should run immediately.
  • scheduleMode: (integer) Mode of the schedule. For broadcasting enter 1, for bursting enter 2
  • runOnceStartDate: (string) Specifiy UTC Date String for start date (ex:'2022-06-07T08:15:46.523Z').
  • recurrence: (array) List of recurrence settings.
    • recurrenceMode: (integer) Mode of recurrence. For hourly recurrence enter 2; for daily recurrence enter 3; for weekly recurrence enter 4 (to specify the day of the week, use true/false operators (ex: onMonday: true); for monthly recurrence enter 5
    • endMode: (object) Settings for ending the recurrence. You can use either endModeRepetitions or endModeDate. Specify the number of times you want the schedule to repeat using the corresponding number (ex: 2, for 2 repetitions of the schedule
      • endModeRepetitions: (integer) Number of repetitions before ending.
      • endModeDate: (string|null) End date for the recurrence, if applicable.
    • onMonday to onSunday: (boolean) Indicates if the schedule runs on the respective day of the week.
    • firstRun: (string) Date and time of the first run in ISO 8601 format.
    • dayCount: (integer) Day on which Monthly recurrence has to run.
  • addStoryLink: (boolean) Enter ‘true’ if you want to add a link to the story and ‘false’ if you do not want to add a link to the story.
  • fileFormat: (string) Enter ‘PDF’ to share a PDF copy of the story or ‘PPT’ to share a PPT format of the story).
  • emailSubject: (string) Subject line for email notifications.
  • emailMessage: (string) Message body for email notifications.
  • deliveryOption: (integer) Specifies the email delivery Option. Enter 0 for Attachment, 1 for Attachment and preview inline, and 2 for Preview Inline
  • timezoneFullName: (string) Full name of the timezone for scheduling.
  • views: (array) List of views to be included in the generated output.
    • bookmarkId: (string) Identifier for the bookmark. Use default “STORY” for scheduling without any bookmark
    • viewName: (string) Name of the view.
    • filename: (string) Name of the file to be generated.
    • fileSettings: (object) Settings for file generation.
      • selectedStoryPages: (array) List of selected pages for the story.
      • includeAppendix: (boolean) Indicates if the appendix should be included.
      • appendixIncludeDate: (boolean) Indicates if the date should be included in the appendix.
      • resolution: (string) Resolution of the output file (e.g., "1920,1080").
      • includeComments: (boolean) Indicates if comments should be included.
      • pageSize: (string) Size of the pages (e.g., "Auto").
    • destinations: (object) Delivery destinations for the file.
      • email: (object) Email delivery settings.
        • extEmailUsr: (array) Enter the email address of the external user/s (ex:abc@gmail.com).
        • internalEmailUsr: (array) Enter the user ID or Team ID of the internal user/s.
  • xsTimeTaken: (integer) Time taken for the execution in milliseconds.

User Authorization through OAuth 3.0 in SAC

Having administrator privileges on our SAC tenant, we can register an OAuth-client in the App Integration tab of the administration page. We navigate to the administration page by clicking on ≡ (Main Menu) -> System -> Administration.

Within the App Integration tab (1), we are now able to register a new OAuth client by clicking on +Add New OAuth Client (2).

This will open the dialog for registering a new OAuth Client as shown below:

Client Configuration Instructions

  1. Client Name: Choose a name for your client.
  2. Purpose: Select “Interactive Usage.”
  3. Redirect URI: Indicate the callback URL that the authorization server will return to after successful authorization.
  4. Token Lifetimes: Defining a lifetime for access and refresh tokens is optional.
  5. Final Steps:
    • Once you provide the required details, ensure to copy the Client ID and Client Secret for use in your application.

Obtaining the Access Token

The diagram below shows the Authorization Code Grant flow for our context and helps you implement it. The end-user authorizes an application to access a protected resource on the Service Provider (SP) and must interact with the IdP (authorization server).

Please note that the SCP Authentication Service is the default IdP but can be changed.

Our custom application should first direct the user to the authorization server's authorization endpoint (Authorization URL). After obtaining the authorization code, the application must exchange it for an access token by making a POST request with the authorization code to the token endpoint (Token URL).

You can find the Authorization URL and Token URL in the App Integration tab under OAuth Clients on SAC's administration page.

Using Postman to Call the Public API

  1. Open Postman and create a new collection.

  2. Go to the Authorization page and fill in the following details to get a new access token:

    • Auth Type: OAuth 2.0
    • Token Name: Provide a suitable token name.
    • Grant Type: Select Authorization Code.
    • Callback URL: Check "Authorize using browser."
    • Auth URL: Use the Authorization URL received from the App Integration page.
    • Client ID: Use the Client ID received while creating the app.
    • Client Secret: Use the client secret obtained in the previous step.
    •   

  3. Click on "Get New Access Token." This will open your browser for user authentication. Once authentication is complete, you will be redirected to Postman with the token.

  4. Use the access token obtained to call the endpoints.

  5. CSRF Token: You will need the CSRF token to call the API endpoints. Use the following code in the Pre-Request script to get and set the csrf_token header:

 

const getAppInstancePropsRequest = {
  url: "https://<tenant-url>/api/v1/csrf",
  method: 'GET',
  header: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' , 'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + pm.collectionVariables.get("bearer.token"), 'x-csrf-token': 'fetch',
  'x-sap-sac-custom-auth': "true" }
};

pm.sendRequest(getAppInstancePropsRequest, function (err, response) {
    console.log(response)
    var csrfToken = response.headers.get("x-csrf-token");
    pm.collectionVariables.set("csrf_token", csrfToken);
})

entry.title: Re: Change the file name of a Custom Form Template PDF (S...

Regards

Dominik

entry.title: Introduction
entry.content:
Hello Everyone

My name is LeSha', and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself:  I'm a former technical business analyst that worked for a brokerage firm, and I have a degree in Information Technology Management.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in supply chain management. I am excited to explore how SAP solutions can streamline operations, enhance visibility across supply chains, and drive efficiency in procurement, logistics, and inventory management. My goal is to build a solid foundation in SAP’s supply chain modules and apply this expertise to help businesses achieve seamless integration and operational excellence.

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because of my passion for technology and my desire to contribute to the public sector by working in government roles. SAP’s ability to streamline complex processes fascinates me, especially in areas like procurement, logistics, and inventory management, which are essential for government operations.

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in technology, business transformation, and supply chain management. I look forward to sharing insights, exchanging knowledge, and collaborating with others on SAP-related topics.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

entry.title: Re: Securing Your Data with Data Access Controls in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi SCN,

in case you did not find an answer yet: Permission table can be shared via view from another space. Access to this space or to edit table content in this space can be restricted.

entry.title: Re: Access to SAP Signavio Process Governance - 30 days Trial Account
entry.content: Hi there - you can get a free trial here: https://editor.signavio.com/p/register

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve data from a fieldymbol nested of deep structures
Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content: Got it, thanks!!

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve data from a fieldymbol nested of deep...
entry.content:
You are looking for a bunch of loops, I think:


DATA:
  lt_data    TYPE ty_table.

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <objects>  TYPE ty_objects,
  <object>   TYPE ty_object,
  <property> TYPE ty_property,
  <basetype> TYPE ty_basetype,
  <line>     TYPE table_line. "ty_table_line.

  LOOP AT lt_data-objects ASSIGNING <objects>.
    LOOP AT <objects>-object ASSIGNING <object>.
      LOOP AT <object>-properties ASSIGNING <property>.
        LOOP AT <property>-property ASSIGNING <basetype>.
          LOOP AT <basetype>-value ASSIGNING <line>.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.

You will replace lt_data with <data> in your example.

entry.title: Error while pulling Branch to Test System in Abap Environment on BTP
entry.content:
Hi experts,

While pulling a branch of a software component to Test system, I got errors "Type /.../CX_RUNTIME is unknown".

In my eclipse ABAP Cloud Project everything is fine and the object exists. In the transport request, there is just one entry for the object /.../CX_RUNTIME (Type APIS). I changed the release state to C1. The class itself is not in the TR. 

I created another TR an put the class /.../CX_RUNTIME itself in, released it and pulled the branch again. I got the same errors.

Does anyone of you know, how to fix this problem?

Thanks and regards, Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Unfortunately, I don't have the financial means to pay for an exam and I would love to participate in this program and perhaps become an officially certified consultant!

entry.title: Modification of tax withholding timing in SAP
entry.content:
I would like to implement withholding tax at the time of incoming invoice posting. Withholding tax should be calculated and recorded during the MIRO transaction (Enter Incoming Invoice).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Modification of tax withholding timing in SAP
entry.content:
You can create your own withholding tax types for Invoice posting if needed.  The withholding tax code and type should be assigned to the Supplier master in app Maintain Business Partner. 

In the help.sap.com documentation 

Review in full for Brazil 
Processing Withholding Tax

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0864cb07010642b3bde45a20de4975bc/fde221053558483c9082ff758bf89aec.html?locale=en-US

See also referenced therein Defining Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0864cb07010642b3bde45a20de4975bc/137b6f7591354b4086b295ef605cd9b2.html?locale=en-US

See also help.sap.com Withholding Tax  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/920bd153c9684608e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

See Example of Withholding Tax in Accounts Payable

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/c347d953189a424de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

See also kba 3383828 Withholding tax not calculated

You are fully responsible for the customizing and postings on your system. Any changes should be thoroughly tested on your test system prior to implementation on production

Rahul

entry.title: 🔵Infuse Generative AI into SAP Business Applications by utilizing Google Cloud Gemini
entry.content:
Generative AI has the potential to improve business processes across a wide range of industries. While SAP ERP products such as S/4HANA are designed to run business processes seamlessly in an automated manner, enterprises can have bottlenecks when business users have to work with unstructured data across distributed systems. 

 

Imagine functionalities such as enterprise search and creating orders in SAP - when search needs to take into account semantic meaning of the search term or when users get an email or a chat request for placing an order, the entire process can be time consuming and error prone.

This is where Google Cloud AI models can help perform these business functions seamlessly.  Google Cloud offers a wide range of AI models including the flagship Gemini family of models, which can provide key GenAI functionalities such as embeddings generation, RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) and multi-modal inputs and responses. These functionalities can be utilized by SAP customers via various SDKs and toolsets, including SAP BTP AI Core and SAP BTP ABAP environment. 

 

In this demo, we will showcase how to build an enterprise application for a sample use case of enterprise search using Google Gemini.

Speakers: 
Ameya Suvarna, Google
Ajith Urimajalu, Google

entry.title: Re: Horizontal scroll in RAP
entry.content: It's exactly Senqi_Hu said, in CDS we don't have anything to do. ADT in eclipse assume table as GridTable. To change it, when you deploy your RAP to fiori app, you have to change manifest.json to change this table of type to generate app as analitic table. In VScode you have a option to change this type of table in moment of generate RAP to fiori app.

entry.title: Re: IBP KF calculation - How to bring calculated values t...
entry.content:
Add a Helper KF LEADTIMENEGATIVE - Leadtime Negative for calculating negative value.

Then use formula for KF1 as IBP_PERIODSHIFT("ZTMPDUMMYKF2@WKPRODLOC","LEADTIMENEGATIVE@WKPRODLOC",''SUM'')

entry.title: SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Sales 2022
entry.content:
Hello, I am preparing for the sales module certification.

I wanted to know if you can guide me on which course to start first. What would be the order of study?

I already saw the video in which they explain the exam topics and each module.

I also already have an exam scheduled for the first week of December,

I have already finished the general course of S 4/HANA and TS_460 Sales part 1.

I have never had experience with SAP before, everything is new to me.

I am self-taught, I signed up for all the courses and downloaded all the books:

1. SAP S_4HANA. Overview (S4H00)

2. SAP S4HANA Sales part 1 (TS460)

3. SAP S4_HANA. (S4600) Business process

4. SAP S4HANA Sales Process (TS4605)

5. SAP S4HANA (TS4610) Delivery Management

6. SAP S4HANA Sales (TS4615) Billing Management

7. SAP S4HANA (TS4620) pricing

8. SAP S4HANA Sales (TS4650) Advanced Customer Support

9. SAP S4HANA Sales (TS4270) Advanced Availability (aATP)

 

What recommendations do you have regarding the order of the courses and any other additional recommendations. Thank you very much in advance.

Observations:

I don't speak English but I'm learning it in Cambridge, my level is only A2, that's why I had to translate this text with Google, I apologize for any errors or poor writing due to this.

greetings,
Maxi

Graziela

entry.title: CORS Issue when Calling CAP Service from HTML5 Application (Non-UI5)
entry.content:
I have an HTML5 application built purely with HTML, JavaScript, and CSS (not on SAP UI5), and I need to call a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) service on SAP BTP. When I try to make the request from the front-end, I'm encountering a CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) issue.

What are the steps to resolve this CORS issue, and how can I configure my CAP service or AppRouter to allow cross-origin requests from my HTML5 app?

Thanks in advance.

#

entry.title: Re: Shared Memory Objects - Transfer data between ABAP programs
entry.content:
@Jelena_Perfiljeva - I believe only short form IMPORT / EXPORT are obsolete and the rest of it lives on. In fact there is an IMPORT and EXPORT to shared memory areas that use the same buffers as Shared objects.

EXPORT - medium - ABAP Keyword Documentation & Shared Objects - Introduction 

@danish_makhdum - From when did SELECT INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS get declared obsolete? In the new syntax just the position within the construct changes... ABAP Help - SELECT INTO CORRESPONDING

entry.title: Re: External API / Web Service integration with SAP Digital Manufacturing - Demystified!
entry.content:
Hi Omer,

Please check if you have marked the service as available in PPD. If not, you won't see it.

Thanks,
Swaroop

entry.title: Re: Determine if there is data in past 24 months of Actuals
entry.content:
Hi @raf_lim,

luckily, this is a lot easier than using as many key figures as you've mentioned. In the following I'm adding the settings of our planning area, so you might need to make some adjustments. 


In this screenshot you see the general setup of the attribute.
We created an attribute for this usecase, with the proper attributes (please adjust it according to your needs). The measure is your actuals qty key figure, base plan level is derived from the actuals (so no inputs needed from your side), but you need to define the calculation level (this should be the same as the keys of the attribute).
Next, choose the periodicity past and 24 months in your case without offset.

This screenshot contains the logic of the xyz segmentation. Herein, you define to aggregate the periods by operator "SUM". That is essentially what you did with your 24 key figures (if I understood you correctly), that means, it sums up the past 24 months of the actuals key figure. By defining the segmentation "(1) by threshold", the system allows you to maintain any segments you like with any summed up value of your requirement.

As written in my previous answer, you need to provide a manipulated actuals key figure. The xyz segmentation by threshold is requiring a value greater than null. So if your actuals has only null values in the past 24 months, you will still not be able to segment it. What you need to do is to create a new key figure with a calculation like the one in the following screenshot. Possibly, a modelling with operator JF() would work as well, but I have not made any experiences on that. There @SubhojitC would might be able to assist you. 

 

In our case, we creating a segment with value "12", if the sum of the past 12 months is 0 or lower. We uses a number instead of a text, because we do not only make this segmentation once but three times for 12, 6 and 3 months without actuals, to provide a more flexible option to filter for business. 

I'm hoping this is giving you all the information necessary. If not, don't hesitate to reach out again.

Best regards,
Matthias

entry.title: Re: RAP: Creating a child entity with 'create by associat...
entry.content: I'd really like the button to be on top of the child entity list (inside of the parent entity page). Is there a way to do this?

entry.title: Visualise end to end progress of Solution using Solution Quotation Fiori application
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, visibility and traceability are critical for effective decision-making. SAP’s Manage Solution Quotation Version 2 app addresses this need by offering a comprehensive view of the entire solution from creation of solution quotation to porgress of each follow-up documents execution. This blog highlighting the new feature and how businesses can leverage it to optimize operations.

From 2023 FPS01, new fiori app 'Manage Solution Quotations - Version 2' is availalbe to use. Using this app user can easily create new solution quotations or search or navigate to existing solution quotations. This app provides a network graph view, allowing user to track the status of a predefined set of follow-up documents such as sales orders, service orders, service contract or subscription contract. At each graphical node, like a sales order or subscription contract, you can see key details including the document ID and status with colour coded node of each follow-ups. A focused progress monitor is available at both the solution quotation header and item levels, offering a clear view of the overall process. Users can also navigate directly to specific documents or transactions, provided they have the necessary roles for access.

On open of this app you will see list of solution quotations created, user can search using the default filters, also can adapt the given filters.

To navigate to the details page of a solution quotation, click on the respective row. To visualize the end-to-end progress of the solution, user must click the "Action" button in the corresponding solution quotation row. On click on action button it opens the fiori object page with Details, Items and Progress Monitor. In this example I have used mixed bundled product which is a combination of different configurable non configurable products. Solution Quotation has been accepted which has generated its follow-up documents( sales order, service order, service contract and subscription contract).

By default, it shows the progress flow of the header documents as depicted in the image above. To visualize a specific item, click on the corresponding line item, To illustrate in this example I have selected item 40, which is a subscription item.

As you progress with specific documents, such as sales documents, you can visualize the further documents like outbound delivery and invoice.

Navigate to Documents from Progress Monitor: To navigate, the user can click on a node, which will display a set of buttons. By clicking the "Navigate to Document" button, the available navigation options(hyperlinks) will be shown depending on the user's authorizations. On click on hyperlink you can navigate from progress monitor to correponding documents. Similalry you can explore other interactive feature by clicking on button like Expand/Collapse, Node Details and Collapse Path.

In summary, the SAP  Manage Solution Quotation version 2 app offers a user-friendly interface for managing and tracking solution quotations. The SAP solution quotation progress monitor enhances business efficiency by providing full visibility into the solution, enabling faster decision-making and improved collaboration. This streamlined process helps organizations reduce errors, accelerate decision making, and optimize resource allocation, driving overall business growth.

Stay tune for my next blog post I will try to explain the other features of Solution Business. I will be grateful to see your feedback on this blog post also let me know if you expect specific blog post on other offering of Manage Solution Quotation application or Solution Business.

entry.title: Re: How to hide transactions in MII?
entry.content:
hi @Private_Member_221638 m

Hope this helps!

Regards,
Swaroop

entry.title: Re: How do I calculate the too new to evaluate, with othe...
entry.content: for the goals too? I did the tests and in objectives the value of the weights of the objectives is being disregarded, the score is being made equal to the behaviors, taking an average.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Need help to configure Group Journal Entry approver workflow on premise
entry.content:
Hi, there are couple of configuration steps you need to complete in order to enable the manage workflow process.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4ebf1502064b406c964b0911adfb3f01/cd047a5505b042c6bc3449982f137f2c.html

You need to create teams and assign functions. All business users who should be assigned as approver or team member needs to be added into the created team from app 'Manage Team and responsibilities'.

The team category is: PGJE_T

The workflow scenarios for manage workflow for group journal entries is: 78500095.

Once the workflow process is enabled correctly, you would be able to create workflow by setting up the condition with document type in Journal entry:

 

 

 

If you need further assistance, you can create a SAP incident or reach to your TQM for MaxAttention service.

entry.title: how to increase the sap cloud alm event alert time window for operations?
entry.content: i'm getting a lot of alerts and i'd like to know if i can reduce them.
